title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NBA cancels Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Monday' promotion after Luke Kornet's open letter raised concerns.
The promotion involved Magic City strip club's chicken wings, merchandise, and a performance by rapper T.I.
Kornet, now with the Spurs, criticized the promotion for not maintaining a family-friendly NBA environment.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal admitted to not knowing who Celtics' guard Baylor Scheierman was during a recent episode of Inside the NBA.
Baylor Scheierman, in his second Celtics season, has averaged 4.8 points and 3.2 rebounds in 59 games this year.
Scheierman has been maintaining his minutes despite Jayson Tatum's return, contributing significantly during Tatum's absence.

title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey calls on Signature Aviation to end collaboration with ICE at Hanscom Field in Bedford.
Signature Aviation aids ICE deportations by providing essential aviation services and access at Hanscom Field.
Healey criticizes ICE's increased deportations under Trump and Signature's role in facilitating these flights.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell 20 feet when a metal grate collapsed outside Boston's Omni Parker House on Friday morning.
Boston Inspectional Services issued a building code violation to the hotel after determining rotted brackets caused the grate's failure.
The hotel was ordered to inspect all vent grates and secure the area while repairs are made, following the incident.

title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Quincy Gas owner Harvey Kertzman seeks a violist to serenade customers at the station on Quincy Avenue.
Kertzman has posted signs for over a year, preferring a violist despite many responses from guitarists.
A retired violist is expected to start performing in warmer weather, with the search ongoing for more musicians.

title: Report: Patriots agree to sign edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots will sign edge rusher Dre'Mont Jones, with the deal reportedly up to $39.5 million over three years.
Dre'Mont Jones, 29, posted seven sacks and 43 tackles last season with the Baltimore Ravens after being acquired from the Titans.
Jones will join the Patriots as his fifth NFL team, bringing a career total of 37.5 sacks over his eight-year career.

title: How a Boston Marathon runner finds purpose in mentoring and miles
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Victoria Walker, 31, from Brighton, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Minds Matter Boston.
Walker mentors two 16-year-olds through Minds Matter Boston, supporting high-achieving students from low-income backgrounds.
Running has provided Walker with purpose and community, complementing her mentoring work and benefiting her mentees.

title: Meet the New England Aquarium’s viral seal and his rubber duckie pal
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Reggae, a harbor seal at New England Aquarium, went viral with a video of him playing with a rubber duck.
The video, featuring cognitive enrichment activities, reached nearly 500,000 views with 100,000 engagements on Instagram.
Trainers use various enrichment activities, like "Find It," to stimulate seals' curiosity and intelligence at the aquarium.

title: What we know about Bravo’s ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 at 9 p.m. on Bravo.
Cast includes a former TV news anchor, a pizzeria co-owner, and Rhode Island's 'Cannabis Queen'.
Red Sox to host show's cast at Fenway Park's Bravo Night on April 17.

title: 24 Boston businesses approved for new or upgraded liquor licenses
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 24 Boston businesses received new or upgraded liquor licenses, enabling them to serve a broader range of alcoholic beverages.
The new licenses are mostly zip code-restricted, targeting neighborhoods like East Boston, Roslindale, and Roxbury for equitable distribution.
Licenses must be returned to the city if not used; approval process is based on neighborhood public need.
